vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited psychiatric services
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 psychotic disorder criteria; clinical assessment with exclusion of substance-induced psychosis

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; affected by treatment gap and adherence barriers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist diagnosis concentrated in district/tertiary hospitals; community recognition variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antipsychotics (generics widely available) with community support where possible; long-acting injectables limited outside tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, delayed care, and limited community rehabilitation services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic antipsychotics and increasing community mental health initiatives in some states
